Smoldering Cudgel  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Blunt Atk Delay: 34
DMG: 18 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +12 MANA: +50
SV FIRE: +25
Effect: Blaze (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 0.8 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Smoldering Cudgel
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Blunt Atk Delay: 34
DMG: 18 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +12 MANA: +50
SV FIRE: +25
Effect: Blaze (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 0.8 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:1H Blunt
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Marten
Lucy Entry By:Icks
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-14 17:45:26
Page Updated:Wed Oct 7th, 2020

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Veeshan's Peak 1.0
NPC Name
Milyex Vioren
Xygoz

Veeshan's Peak 2.0
NPC Name
Milyex Vioren


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Veeshan's Peak 1.0
Veeshan's Peak 2.0

Modern UI inspect window - Smoldering Cudgel

Uploaded October 6th, 2020 by Andhrimnir
Send a correction
Post Comment
Totally insane on my monk
QuoteReply
# Apr 02 2023 at 4:54 PM Rating: Decent
Solithis
6 posts
So, my TLP guild decided to some Veeshan's Peak and this puppy dropped from Xygoz. Now, I have the aged version from VP 2.0 on a 60 monk on regular server. The aged version melts mobs so much that it's impossible for a merc tank to pull aggro from me. With that in mind I decided to unload my DKP for this. Everybody was looking at the ratio I ended up picking it up for the minimum bid. After the raid, I decided to kill some juggernauts in Sebilis with a guild group.
I was fully aware from experience of what this club could do and needless to say everybody in the group went "WTF?!".
Here's the rundown: on a 60 monk with maxed triple attack (with 41% haste + epic haste -> 81% haste in total) and 255 DEX this thing procs an average of 10 times on a higher hitpoint mob like a juggernaut. It never procs less than 6 or 7 times on any single mob down there. I would regularly have 13-15 procs in a fight and would spend half the fight FD to give the tank a break. The group was of course extremely happy (as was I) because those juggs would completely melt one after another. On raids however... this thing will kill you and may get you guildkicked in instant if it procs like it normally does. Absolutely insane, both the aged and the regular version.
▲ Page top

# Jul 15 2001 at 11:47 PM Rating: Default

1 post
Come on now, this is the fastest dam/delay ratio 1h bl weapon in the game at 1.888, every single class listed would want to use this weapon, and with a weight of 0.8 there is virtually no stamina loss. with a Wis: +12 and Mana: +50 its effectivly +18 wis at level 50 and +16wis at level 60. while the delay is high with a haste item and shaman or enchanter haste on with a dex buff for procs anyone using it would do some serious hurt. I cant see any class that can use it turning it down.

Who it's good for
QuoteReply
# Apr 12 2001 at 10:18 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It's good for every single class shown there.

As for the delay.. apparently all you people talking about the delay of this weapon haven't ever heard of a weapon called the "Wurmslayer."

Quit being greedy. Whoever gets this weapon will put it to great use, regardless of which class they happen to be.

Of course, as I'm a warrior and have no use for wis or mana, I'd let any of the wisdom types get this before I demand a random on it. But if it's a shadow knight, rogue, or monk, we're rolling.

And no, the wu stick won't outdamage this. Not with that proc.

Stat monks
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2001 at 9:21 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This item is great for monks that beef up stats like Dex and Str. Primarily dex for this item. As someone posted, this proc is a level 34 mage spell that does around 300direct damage. With about 120 or 130 dex (easily attainable by just about any monk) you'd probably see this thing proc 5 or 6 times a battle. The only bad thing about that is the taunt factor. Maybe it should go to the warriors firstly. You certainly don't want that kind of a taunt on your cleric LOL. Wouldn't mind it as a druid though. Shamen really shouldn't care about taunt factor as they have far more AC than druids and a pet usually to re-aggro the MOB. Ok, lots of wasted white space...TTYL folks.

Rebutal (-:
QuoteReply
# Apr 10 2001 at 9:34 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The last statement was legitimate, but while it stated that clerics have it easy getting wis maxed, it left out the fact that monks have it easy when it comes to finding a weapon better than this. A ranger, on the other hand, might not be able to find a weapon with as good a ratio as a monk could, and also benifits from the wisdom and mana. And of course the effect is helpful for both classes. My opinion is that it would be best in the hands of a Ranger or Paly, especially the Ranger as Paladins use slashing weapons more often, and also use 2 handers more often as they cannot duel wield.
In the end, it really matters who WOULD get the best use out of it, and not who COULD get the best use, as a monk might not have a good ratio weapon and the Ranger does have a high wisdom weapon, and the cleric has a low wis item.

▲ Page top
RE: no im not a monk
QuoteReply
# Apr 10 2001 at 9:45 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This is the person who posted "Rebutal (:". I feel the first RE: to "no im not a monk" is inaccurate. In order to be able to duel wield as often as you can I believe you need as fast a weapon in your secondery melee as you can aquire. But the primary having a slow delay and a high dmg seems to be a good thing to me by the time you may aquire it. If you could aquire this you would most likely be in a guild that IMHO would have you with a high haste item, possibly a casted haste item (I.E. Monk epic, rogue epic... ect.), and could keep you in a group that most often had an enc/sham casting haste, plus possibly being with a bard. This means your high haste percentage could very likely be halfway useless with a low dly weapon, as your dly would peak at 8 (I'm fairly positive that is the absolute lowest you can achieve). See my point? If you don't, plz don't rough me up too bad.
▲ Page top
RE: no im not a monk
QuoteReply
# Nov 19 2001 at 8:07 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
No, actually the "first re:no i'm not a monk" guy is correct, and you are mistaken. Many people have gone to great lengths to prove that secondary hand delay is independent of primary delay. If you have a 19 delay in primary, and a 34 delay weapon is seconday, the primary will swing every 1.9 seconds. Every 3.4 seconds, the dual wield check will be made, and if it succeeds, the other weapon will swing.

Now, as if this were not enough, the other reason you want your fastest weapon in primary is that your primary weapon benefits from both the level based damage bonus, and the strength bonus. The offhand hits for the weapon damage only. As an example, my level 17 warrior has an 11dmg/29 dly weapon in primary, and a 10 damage/29 delay in secondary (Icetooth claws, Blackened alloy LS). The primary weapon hits for max hit of about 26 or 27, as I have 148 strength unbuffed, and usually have a strength buff. Even with my buffed strength (182 was highest to date, with storm strength) I still hit for 20 (or 22, don't recall) max on offhand. When he gets higher level, and the level based damage bonus starts, the difference becomes larger. So what you want is the fast weapon in primary, to get the damage bonuses as many hits as possible, and the high damage, slow weapon in secondary. You'll get more damage output this way.

Last point, Unless you have an Incredibly fast weapon, you won't have to worry much about hitting the minimum delay cap.

Hope this helps!
▲ Page top
